purpose: The commit addresses a bug in the Bluetooth hci_sync module by adding a check for the HCI_UNREGISTER flag before queuing commands. This prevents a use-after-free (UAF) error that would occur if the device was unregistered.
The commit removes an unnecessary include of asm/termios.h in n_hdlc.c, improving code cleanliness and avoiding potential complications.
The commit updates the documentation for the perf c2c tool to include a new 'N/A' metric in store operations. It modifies two sections of the documentation to explain this new metric.
This commit addresses issues in vma lock handling during split and unmapping operations, fixing race conditions and resource management problems.

The commit updates the devicetree bindings for the rt5682s codec to document AVDD and MICVDD power supply requirements. It adds these properties to ensure proper configuration of the codec's power supplies in device trees.
The commit addresses a resource leak in the zorro7xx_remove_one() function by adding an iounmap() call to properly release resources that were previously not freed.
The commit removes an unused 'work' field from the struct mlx5e_async_ctx to clean up the code.

The commit changes the key_idx field from an unsigned 8-bit integer (u8) to a signed 8-bit integer (s8) in the cfg80211_auth_request struct. This change allows key_idx to hold negative values, specifically -1, which was causing warnings from static checkers like smatch when assigned.
The commit sorts the CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS configuration option in defconfig files to match savedefconfig's order, with no functional changes.

This commit introduces a helper function mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode() to check if the chip is in CAN-FD mode or listen-only mode. It replaces open-coded checks in multiple places with this new function, improving code maintainability and readability.

The commit modifies the rtc test suite to skip tests when the RTC device is not present. It adds checks in multiple test functions to detect if the RTC file descriptor could not be opened due to ENOENT (file not found) error and skips those tests with a descriptive message.
Removes an unnecessary variable assignment that was causing a compiler warning.
The commit modifies the configuration and code to allow rcuscale to function without requiring RCU Tasks. This change removes unnecessary dependencies, reducing kernel size and complexity.
The commit corrects a duplicated phrase 'be split' in a comment within spte.h, improving code clarity.

The commit reverts a previous change that populated the dcn315 clock table based on dcfclk due to issues caused by pmfw filling the table incorrectly when dcfclk states were disabled. This led to underflow situations, so the revert prevents this issue until a better solution is implemented.

The commit updates the Mali-Bifrost GPU device tree bindings to include a compatible string for the Renesas RZ/V2L SoC (R9A07G054). This allows the GPU driver to correctly identify and support this specific hardware.
The commit renames the struct pcie_port to dw_pcie_rp across multiple files in the DWC PCIe driver to maintain consistent naming conventions.
The commit addresses a clang warning related to incorrect format specifiers in debug messages. It changes the format string to correctly handle integer promotions, fixing a known issue.

The commit addresses a bug in the MIPS jump label implementation where negative branch offsets triggered unnecessary warnings. The fix involves casting the upper bound of the branch range to a long type to ensure correct signed comparison, preventing false warnings.
The commit removes an unnecessary case from SetHwReg8188EU() by directly calling the relevant function, simplifying the code and eliminating redundant layers.
The commit addresses sparse warnings by declaring non-global symbols as static in the arm64 kernel code. This change prevents unintended external linkage of these symbols, improving code quality.
This commit fixes an incorrect specification of interrupt flags in the MDSS nodes by removing them, as the provider only uses a single cell for interrupts.

The commit modifies how arrays are allocated in struct ctrl_ref to prepare for dynamic array size changes from drivers. It involves renaming variables and functions related to array allocation and error handling.

The commit adds support for additional PMICs (Power Management Integrated Circuits) and SoCs (System on Chips) in the socinfo driver. Specifically, it includes new entries for models like SM8350, SC8280XP, SA8540P, and others, allowing the system to recognize these devices properly.

The commit improves the handling of chip versions by recognizing all possible cuts (A-P) instead of stopping at E and showing 'unknown'. It simplifies the logging code to directly map chip_cut values to letters.
The commit removes the custom software enable count for PRCMU clocks since the core already handles this, simplifying the code.
The commit aims to clean up and refactor the use of #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL in the RISC-V mm initialization code by grouping similar cases together, reducing conditional compilation directives.

The commit enables QUP (Quicksilver Processing Unit) and GPI DMA (General Purpose Input/Output Direct Memory Access) hardware components in the device tree for the sm6350-lena platform. This change is preparatory work to allow I2C nodes to function properly in future updates.
The commit improves code comments by correcting terminology and formatting.

This commit fixes an infinite loop that occurs when releasing software-firmware synchronization if the hardware semaphore cannot be acquired, typically due to unexpected hardware removal. The fix involves checking for the failure to acquire the semaphore and logging an error before exiting instead of looping indefinitely.

The commit updates the pcm3168a driver to use more modern ASoC DAI format terminology by replacing 'master_mode' with 'provider_mode' and updating related switch cases. This change aligns the code with current standards without introducing new functionality or fixing known bugs.

The commit removes redundant code related to the IS_SWAPFILE macro in NFS file handling. The code was added as a hack for future NFS swap support but is now unnecessary because IS_SWAPFILE is always defined.

This commit implements handling of GTP echo requests by adding functionality to send appropriate echo responses. It adds code for both GTPv0 and GTPv1u protocols, including validation of incoming echo requests and constructing the corresponding echo response packets with necessary information elements.

The commit replaces the use of cpumask_next_* functions with cpumask_first_* where appropriate. It introduces a new function cpumask_first_zero() to handle cases where no next bit is set. The changes aim to improve code efficiency and readability by using more suitable functions for finding the first CPU in a mask.

The commit removes the call to 'dtim_assoc' IOVAR because it's no longer supported by the firmware, which was causing failures during roaming. This change prevents error messages from appearing on the console.
The commit adds support for a platform without an amplifier by introducing a new board configuration (adl_rt5682) that connects the alc5682 codec to SSP0, following Intel's BT offload design.
The commit adds a check to fail zerocopy sendmsg operations if the socket does not support zero-copy. It ensures that such operations are properly handled by returning an error when unsupported.

The commit modifies a LaTeX preamble file to check for the presence of xeCJK.sty before loading it. This addresses issues where the absence of this package could cause errors during PDF documentation generation, particularly on certain distributions.
The commit modifies checkpatch.pl to allow macros that use 'case' without triggering an error. It adds an exception in the script to prevent false positives for valid code.

This commit refactors the simpledrm driver to use atomic helpers instead of simple-KMS helpers. It replaces the existing pipe-based implementation with separate plane, CRTC, and encoder components, improving code structure and maintainability without changing functionality.

The commit introduces a JPEG decoding worker interface to manage multiple hardware instances, enabling parallel processing on MT8195. It adds functions for hardware resource allocation, task distribution, and work management.
This commit removes unnecessary VPRINTK() debug print statements and the ata_scsi_dump_cdb function from the libata driver, as their information is already covered by tracepoints or deemed redundant.

This commit adds support for entering low-power states (zstate) during extended vblank periods when using FreeSync. It modifies various components to enable this optimization, including checks for zstate support and adjustments to display timings.

The commit corrects a type mismatch causing a Control Flow Integrity (CFI) violation in the hqd_destroy callback. The reset_type parameter was incorrectly declared as uint32_t but should be enum kfd_preempt_type.
The commit corrects a typo in the device tree bindings for the STM32 pinctrl driver, changing 'reset' to 'resets'. It also adds a schema constraint to prevent additional properties.
The commit addresses an array-bounds warning in GCC 12 by modifying build flags to suppress the warning.

The commit replaces pm_runtime_get_sync with pm_runtime_resume_and_get to simplify the power management handling in the sun8i-ss PRNG driver. This change streamlines the code by removing unnecessary error handling steps.

This commit reorganizes code related to fixed file installation and removal within the io_uring subsystem. It moves helper functions to the appropriate file (filetable.c) and adjusts their parameters to use context instead of request objects, improving code structure and maintainability.

This commit modifies the device tree bindings for the NXP iMX8MQ VPU by splitting G1 and G2 decoder blocks into separate nodes. It updates the compatible properties, reg, interrupts, and clocks to support independent configuration of each decoder block with proper power-domain management.
The commit removes an unused macro (IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON) and inlines its contents into another macro (IWL_DEVICE_BZ). This is a refactoring change to simplify the code structure.
